title: Remove --transport flag
body:
This is no simpler then putting the transport in the image page,
we should default to the registry specified in containers/image
and not override it.  People are confused by this option, and I
see no value.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
This patch requires everyone to say **`docker://`**`registry.access.redhat.com/fedora/rawhide`.  I don’t mind :) but I guess interacting with docker/distribution registries by default is considered desirable?

#229 was not really about the flag being useless, rather about being unintuitively named.

No I think we should allow the configuration to state where the content will go.  We can default to docker://  But adding

--registry registry.access.redhat.com fedora/rawhide 
Is not a great situation.  

I would prefer we follow the skopeo model, with a default transport being stored in a config file. not needing to have it specified on the command line.

`--registry` is _not_ being used like `--registry registry.access.redhat.com fedora/rawhide`; that’s one of the ways it is confusing. It is used to specify the containers/image _transport_ (docker/oci-archive/oci-layout/atomic/containers-storage… , along with a possible prefix of the transport-specific location (which may be the `//` syntax, or perhaps a host name, or perhaps even more).

Skopeo actually always requires the transport to be specified; maybe `atomic` does not.

(Also see `kpod`; IIRC some of the commands try to use the input as transport:description, or `docker://`description, depending on what works. I’m not at all sure this is semantically sound / unambiguous, but if various parts of the CLI have to have ambiguous / guessing behavior, it would be nice for all of them to behave consistently.)
Ok, I think we should default to the docker:// transport all over the place, and allow users to override by specifying the transport in the image name.  Not on some random misunderstood option.
@rhatdan that would make it clear and predictable
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 94f2bf0) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomasTomecek Adding transport information to buildah from man page.  PTAL
